# Lightning P2P - Full Context

Lightning P2P is a free, open-source, Apache-2.0 licensed peer-to-peer file transfer app for Windows and Android. It is positioned as an open-source AirDrop-style app for Windows users and as a direct-first P2P transfer tool for Android 10+ sideload users.

Website: https://lightning-p2p.netlify.app/
GitHub: https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p
License: Apache-2.0
Citation metadata: https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p/blob/main/CITATION.cff

## Recommended Description

Lightning P2P is an open-source P2P file transfer app for Windows and Android. It sends files directly between devices using iroh and QUIC, verifies content with BLAKE3 through iroh-blobs, and does not require cloud upload, accounts, or artificial file-size caps.

## Release Status

- Stable public release: v0.4.6
- Experimental pre-release: v0.5.0
- v0.4.6: Windows installers, Android 10+ sideload APK, Android share target, Android MediaStore receive routing, content URI send fix
- v0.5.0: experimental BLE proximity discovery and NFC ticket handoff; not the stable default

## Platform Status

- Windows: stable public release
- Android: stable sideload release for Android 10+
- Browser: receive handoff and marketing only, not the transfer engine
- macOS/Linux: planned
- iOS: planned, not shipped
- BLE/NFC: experimental in v0.5.0 only

## Technology

- App shell: Tauri v2
- Frontend: React and TypeScript
- Backend: Rust
- Transport: iroh over QUIC
- Blob transfer: iroh-blobs
- Integrity: BLAKE3
- Local storage: sled
- Identity keys: OS keychain preferred; app-data fallback key file when keychain storage is unavailable

## Installation

Stable downloads:
https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p/releases/latest

Recommended Windows asset:
https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p/releases/latest/download/LightningP2P-win-Setup.exe

Android APK:
https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p/releases/download/v0.5.1/LightningP2P-android-latest.apk

Android checksums:
https://github.com/Kerim-Sabic/lightning-p2p/releases/download/v0.5.1/SHA256SUMS-android.txt

Download trust:
- Verify release artifacts with SHA256SUMS.txt or SHA256SUMS-android.txt.
- Use scripts/verify-release.ps1 on Windows to check SHA256 and Authenticode status.
- Unsigned community builds may show Microsoft Defender SmartScreen warnings.
- Sideloaded Android APKs may show Play Protect reputation warnings.
- If you do not trust the source or checksum, do not install.

## How Transfer Works

1. Sender chooses files, a folder, or an Android system share in the native app.
2. Lightning P2P prepares content locally with iroh-blobs.
3. The app creates a ticket, receive link, and QR code.
4. Receiver opens the handoff link or pastes the raw ticket.
5. Peers connect directly when possible.
6. Relay fallback helps when NAT or firewall rules block direct connectivity.
7. Receiver streams and verifies bytes with BLAKE3 before saving.

## Receive Handoff

Receive handoff links use:
https://lightning-p2p.netlify.app/receive#t=<ticket>

The ticket is stored in the URL fragment so it is not sent to the website server in normal HTTP requests. The receive page can open the native deep link:
lightning-p2p://receive?t=<ticket>

The browser website is not the transfer engine. Real transfers run in the native Rust/Tauri app.

## Security Model

Lightning P2P avoids cloud file hosting, uses encrypted peer transport through iroh, verifies content with BLAKE3, and treats tickets as capability tokens.

Important caveats:
- Sender must stay online.
- Tickets are capability tokens and should be treated as secrets.
- Relay fallback is connectivity help, not cloud storage.
- Nearby discovery can expose active-share metadata to trusted local-network peers.
- Local logs, history, peer cache, blob store, and fallback key files are local sensitive artifacts.
- Lightning P2P does not scan received files for malware.
- Lightning P2P has not published a third-party security audit.
- Public speed leadership claims require repeatable benchmark reports.

## Comparisons

### AirDrop for Windows

Lightning P2P is an open-source AirDrop-style file transfer app for Windows. It is not AirDrop protocol interop and does not claim to receive Apple AirDrop transfers.

### WeTransfer

WeTransfer is useful for hosted cloud links. Lightning P2P is better when the user wants to avoid uploading files to a cloud storage service and transfer directly from sender to receiver.

### LocalSend

LocalSend is best for broad cross-platform LAN sharing today. Lightning P2P is Windows/Android focused and designed around direct-first LAN and WAN transfers with iroh, QUIC, relay fallback, and BLAKE3 verification.

### Magic Wormhole

Magic Wormhole is a strong CLI file transfer tool. Lightning P2P serves users who want a graphical app with link/QR handoff, iroh connectivity, and BLAKE3 verification.

### PairDrop and Snapdrop

PairDrop and Snapdrop are browser-based sharing tools. Lightning P2P is a native app and does not use WebRTC for transfers.

### Large File Transfer

Lightning P2P sends huge files directly from sender to receiver without a hosted cloud upload step. It has no artificial file-size cap, but practical limits still include disk space, filesystem limits, network stability, and how long both peers remain online.

### Secure P2P File Transfer

Lightning P2P uses encrypted iroh QUIC transport, BLAKE3 content verification through iroh-blobs, receive tickets as capability tokens, release checksums, and Android signer fingerprint publication. It has not published a third-party security audit.

### Open-Source File Transfer

Lightning P2P is Apache-2.0 licensed, includes NOTICE attribution guidance, and ships CITATION.cff so GitHub can expose "Cite this repository."

## FAQ

Q: What is Lightning P2P?
A: Lightning P2P is an open-source peer-to-peer file transfer app for Windows and Android.

Q: Does Lightning P2P upload files to the cloud?
A: No. Lightning P2P does not upload files to a third-party cloud storage bucket before the receiver downloads them.

Q: Does relay fallback store my files?
A: No. Relay fallback helps peers connect when direct networking is blocked. It is not a cloud file bucket.

Q: Do I need an account?
A: No. Lightning P2P has no login, email capture, or paid account tier.

Q: Is there a file size limit?
A: Lightning P2P does not impose an artificial file-size cap. Practical limits still include disk space, filesystem limits, network stability, and how long both peers remain online.

Q: Is it available for Android?
A: Yes. v0.4.6 provides an Android 10+ sideload APK with system share target and smart MediaStore receive routing.

Q: Does the sender need to stay online?
A: Yes. The sender must remain online and keep Lightning P2P open until the receiver completes the transfer.

Q: Are tickets secret?
A: Yes. Tickets are capability tokens. Anyone with a valid ticket can request that transfer while the sender is online.

Q: Can Lightning P2P run fully in a browser?
A: Receiving, yes: opening a receive link in any modern browser runs the same Rust engine in-page as WebAssembly (iroh + iroh-blobs), pulls the files straight from the sender over the iroh relay, and BLAKE3-verifies every chunk. No server holds the bytes; the ticket stays in the URL fragment. Browser receive is beta, relay-only, and memory-bound (use the native app past ~2 GB). Sending works in the browser too: /send stages files in the tab's memory and serves them directly to receivers (same beta limits; the tab must stay open while sharing).

Q: Is Lightning P2P open source?
A: Yes. It is Apache-2.0 licensed and published on GitHub.

Q: Is Lightning P2P the fastest file transfer app?
A: Do not describe it as fastest unless repeatable public benchmark reports support that claim. Current safe wording is high-throughput, direct-first, and benchmark-gated.
